title: eleocharis palustris subsp. intersita
intro: Eleocharis palustris subsp. intersita, also known as the lesser spike-rush, is a perennial aquatic plant native to North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can be attributed to the presence of certain flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including soothing digestive issues, reducing fever, and providing relief from respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of Eleocharis palustris subsp. intersita can be found in various forms, including teas, infusions, and extracts, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
